title: Sunset Dunes San Francisco: Complete Visitor Guide
content: # Your Complete Guide to Sunset Dunes: San Francisco’s Newest Oceanfront Park. **Whether you’re taking in the majesty of the Golden Gate Bridge, walking beneath the redwoods of Muir Woods, or exploring the beauty of Wine Country, Dylan’s Tours connects you to the heart and soul of San Francisco—and beyond.**. *Sunset Dunes is San Francisco’s newest coastal park, a free, two-mile oceanfront promenade along Ocean Beach on the city’s western edge. It opened April 12, 2025, runs from Lincoln Way to Sloat Boulevard, and is open daily and accessible by bike, foot, or Muni.*. There’s a whole other edge of San Francisco that most tourists never reach, and it’s one of the most beautiful stretches of coastline in California. Sunset Dunes runs two miles along Ocean Beach with the Pacific stretching out on one side and the low-slung houses of the Outer Sunset neighborhood on the other.

title: Sunset Dunes Park, San Francisco, California, United States — Park Review | Condé Nast Traveler
content: It’s less a traditional park and more a coastal corridor for relaxed moments of movement and connection to the elements. Public art installations dot the route, adding moments of surprise and play, while sandy access points lead directly onto San Francisco's Ocean Beach. title: What to see at Sunset Dunes, S.F.’s new Great Highway park
content: # 10 ways to explore Sunset Dunes, S.F.’s new park along the former Great Highway. San Francisco’s newest recreation area has been many things in the city’s history: sand dunes, a hunting ground, a cluster of streetcars used as housing, a highway and now a contentious flashpoint of a park. But there’s one place where the vibes are immaculate right now: The actual two miles and 50 acres of Sunset Dunes, spanning along Ocean Beach from Sloat Boulevard to Lincoln Way. I spent two days there before the park’s grand opening, and found one delightful conversation after another — with dog walkers, muralists, Sunset locals, bicyclists from out of town and one history-loving bathroom attendant. Sloat Boulevard and Great Highway, S.F. The Friends of Ocean Beach Park are a force in Sunset Dunes, welcoming visitors, organizing cleanups and entertainment, and raising $400,000 to commission more than a dozen rotating public art pieces and murals along the road.
